authoritative_sources.title: Child Care and Development Block Grant Act (CCDBG) of 2014: Plain Language Summary of Statutory Changes
authoritative_sources.publisher: Administration for Children and Families, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal program authority summary naming child care inspection cadence, transparency, background checks, training, and ratio expectations that shape the lane's compliance baseline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Licensing baseline and rule hierarchy intake

authoritative_sources.title: What Is Child Care Licensing?
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ACF guidance defining licensed and license-exempt care, minimum health-safety requirements, and monitoring posture for regulated child care.

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring and Inspections
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ACF guidance that identifies monitoring and inspection reports, health-safety violations, corrective actions, and substantiated complaints as core licensing artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Background Checks
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ACF guidance describing covered workers, required check components, and the five-year cadence for child care background checks.

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Qualifications and Required Training
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ACF guidance naming training topics and qualification evidence that commonly underpin licensing readiness and corrective-action closure.

authoritative_sources.title: CCDBG Background Check and Health and Safety Training Requirements (CCDF-ACF-PIQ-2017-01)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Administration for Children and Families, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Office of Child Care guidance clarifying scope boundaries for licensed, regulated, registered, and license-exempt providers and clarifying training expectations before unsupervised care.

authoritative_sources.title: Starting a Child Care Business
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care State Capacity Building Center / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Office of Child Care technical-assistance resource that points operators to state licensing agencies and program regulations, reinforcing state licensing agencies and rule sets as operational sources of truth.

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Our Children overview
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Resource Center for Health and Safety in Child Care and Early Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized national early-childhood health and safety standards used to tighten terminology and evidence expectations after primary regulatory sources.

workflow_stages.name: Licensing baseline and provider-type intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify jurisdiction, licensing authority, provider type, and the applicable public rule hierarchy before issuing any compliance conclusion.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map child care licensing obligations to the exact records, evidence owners, and review cadence required for the current provider context.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Monitoring, inspection, and evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Assess inspection findings, complaint outcomes, training and background-check records, and supporting health-safety evidence for completeness and freshness.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Corrective-action and remediation handling
workflow_stages.description: Convert findings, missing evidence, and operational-policy conflicts into remediation packages with explicit owners and blocked-decision logic.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and signoff escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refuse legal, regulator-facing, background-check adjudication, or formal signoff work and route it to the appropriate owner with preserved evidence context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Licensing application, renewal, and provider profile records
License status records and licensing file notes
Substantiated complaint records and deficiency notices
Corrective action plans and remediation trackers
Staff rosters, role assignments, and ratio or supervision support records
Background-check clearance records and household-member screening records
Training, qualification, CPR, first-aid, and safe-sleep records
Incident, emergency drill, medication, and health-safety logs used as licensing evidence
Review cadence schedules, approval-owner maps, and escalation notes
systems_of_record: State or territorial licensing portal or provider database
Tenant document management or compliance evidence repository
Background-check tracking system or clearance registry
Training registry or learning management repository
Corrective-action tracker and approval workflow system

allowed: Map child-care-specific obligations to concrete evidence, owners, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness, gap, and remediation summaries for licensing-related work.
Escalate conflicts, missing prerequisites, and adjacent-authority requests without overstating authority.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, provider-type, licensing status, or source-of-truth context.
Conflicting public guidance, licensing authority expectations, and tenant policy.
Requests that imply legal interpretation, regulator-facing response, background-check adjudication, or safety/executive authority.
refuse: Any legal advice, regulator negotiation, or formal licensing opinion.
Formal signoff on corrective-action closure, waivers, renewals, or certification of compliance.
Incident severity determination, child-safety decisioning, or criminal-history adjudication outside lane authority.

domain_failure_modes: Treating child care as generic compliance work and failing to request licensing status, provider type, or inspection records.
Marking readiness complete without required background-check, household-member, or training evidence.
Ignoring the difference between center-based and family-child-care artifacts and therefore missing required home-based controls.
Treating public summaries or tenant practice as sufficient when jurisdiction-specific licensing rules could change the outcome.
Allowing operational convenience to override documented policy, inspection findings, or review cadence.
Providing regulator-facing or legal conclusions without escalating to the proper authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: State, tribal, territorial, and local licensing rules vary materially across provider types and can change inspection, training, and staffing evidence expectations.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change whether a control is satisfied, missing, or not applicable.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat as non-blocking only because the lane requires jurisdiction and provider-type retrieval before acting and refuses to guess when the answer would change.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Some providers operate under overlapping contexts such as subsidy participation, Head Start, public school pre-k, or other exempt arrangements with different rule overlays.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change the applicable evidence set, escalation owner, and whether licensing rules apply directly.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval of program model and controlling authority and escalate when overlapping rule sets materially conflict.

notes: The lane remains validated because the unresolved ambiguity is handled through required runtime retrieval and explicit escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.
Research constrains the pack to child-care-specific licensing records, monitoring artifacts, and authority boundaries rather than generic compliance language.
